summary: I was born in Hopkinsville, Kentucky, though my family moved around a lot because my dad was in the military. We lived there during the first five years of my life before moving back to Georgia. Hopkinsville is where my early roots began before my journey through dental school and eventually settling in other places.

After moving around a bit in my early years, my family settled back in Georgia when I was five. I later attended dental school at the Medical College of Georgia, which was a significant step in my career. Georgia was an important place for my education and early life before moving to Charlotte.

I went to dental school at the Medical College of Georgia, which was where I trained to become a general dentist. This education laid the foundation for my career, and after graduating, I chose Charlotte, North Carolina, as my home for many years. The Medical College of Georgia played a crucial role in shaping my professional path.

Charlotte became my home for twenty-four years after dental school. My wife and I ran two pediatric dentistry practices there, and it was where I met her and eventually proposed. Charlotte was a growing city with a strong dental community, and it was a place full of professional and personal milestones for us.

I was born and raised in Lexington, Kentucky, growing up in some of the tougher neighborhoods and attending Bryan Station High School. Later, I went to the University of Kentucky for dental school and completed a residency program there. Lexington shaped much of my early life and education before I moved on to other places for specialization and work.

I graduated from Bryan Station High School in Lexington, Kentucky, which was part of my challenging upbringing. Despite attending what were considered some of the worst schools, I persevered and went on to college with an engineering scholarship. Bryan Station was an important starting point in my educational journey.

I completed my dental degree at the University of Kentucky and also did a two-year hospital dentistry residency there. I worked with many complex patients, including those with infectious diseases and bone marrow transplants. Later, I joined the faculty but faced tenure challenges that influenced my decision to move to Charlotte.

I pursued my master's degree and specialty in pediatric dentistry at Ohio State University after deciding to focus on working with children. This advanced training helped me transition from hospital dentistry to pediatric dentistry, which I found more fulfilling. Ohio State was a key step in my professional specialization.

Charlotte was where I built my pediatric dentistry career and met my wife. The city was growing fast and had a good demand for pediatric dentists, which made it an ideal place for us professionally. We owned two practices there before deciding to sell and move to Mount Sterling, Kentucky.

Richmond, Virginia, was one of the cities I considered when looking for faculty positions or private practice opportunities after my residency and specialization. However, I ultimately chose Charlotte over Richmond because of the city's growth and opportunities. Richmond was part of my decision-making process but not where I settled.

Indiana was another location I considered for career opportunities in pediatric dentistry or academia. Like Richmond and Georgia, it was part of my search for the right place to establish my practice. Ultimately, I did not choose Indiana but kept it in mind during my decision process.

Atlanta, Georgia, was among the southeastern cities I looked at for potential faculty or private practice opportunities in pediatric dentistry. Although it had a dental school and was a large city, I decided to go with Charlotte instead. Atlanta was part of the regional context during my career planning.

Texas was another state I explored for career opportunities, including Baylor University. I looked at several places across the southeast and beyond before settling on Charlotte. Texas was part of my broader search but not where I ultimately established my practice.

Baylor University in Texas was one of the institutions I considered when looking for faculty positions or private practice opportunities. It was part of my evaluation of potential places to continue my career after completing my specialty training. Although I did not end up there, Baylor was on my radar.

We moved to Mount Sterling, Kentucky, in August 2024 as part of our plan to slow down and transition out of private practice. It’s been a few months since we settled here, and we got licensed in Kentucky. Mount Sterling is where The Post Clinic is located, just minutes from our home, and it’s been a perfect fit for us to give back to the community.

The Post Clinic is a wonderful place that we discovered after moving to Mount Sterling. We started volunteering there because it felt like family, with a strong sense of community and a mission to provide respectful, quality dental care to those in need. Working there on Mondays and Tuesdays, and even visiting on other days, has been incredibly rewarding and fulfilling for both of us.
